name: api-service
description: Backend API service docs with an emphasis on docs/api/: per-endpoint reference pages with request/response examples, auth, and error contracts.
structure:
```text
mkdocs.yml                     <- MkDocs config (see the mkdocs.yml template)
docs/
  index.md                     <- Service overview, base URL, auth model
  architecture.md              <- Request lifecycle + component diagram
  setup.md                     <- Run the service locally + call it
  api/                         <- THE FOCUS: endpoint reference pages
    index.md                   <- Endpoint index grouped by resource, auth notes
    <resource>.md              <- One page per resource/endpoint group
  guides/
    index.md                   <- Guides index
    authentication.md          <- Auth flow, tokens, scopes
    errors.md                  <- Error format + status-code conventions
.magestic-ai/.docgen.json      <- {last_run, head_sha, scope_hash}
```
mkdocs_yml:
```yaml
site_name: <service name from package.json/pyproject.toml or directory name>
site_description: <one-line API description from README or CLAUDE.md>
docs_dir: docs

theme:
  name: material
  features:
    - navigation.tabs
    - navigation.sections
    - navigation.expand
    - navigation.top
    - search.suggest
    - search.highlight
    - content.code.copy
  palette:
    - media: "(prefers-color-scheme: light)"
      scheme: default
      toggle:
        icon: material/weather-sunny
        name: Switch to dark mode
    - media: "(prefers-color-scheme: dark)"
      scheme: slate
      toggle:
        icon: material/weather-night
        name: Switch to light mode

markdown_extensions:
  - admonition
  - pymdownx.details
  - pymdownx.superfences:
      custom_fences:
        - name: mermaid
          class: mermaid
          format: !!python/name:pymdownx.superfences.fence_code_format
  - pymdownx.tabbed:
      alternate_style: true
  - tables
  - toc:
      permalink: true

nav:
  - Home: index.md
  - Architecture: architecture.md
  - Setup: setup.md
  - API Reference: api/index.md
  - Guides: guides/index.md
```
page_templates:
### `docs/index.md`

- One-paragraph "what this service does"
- Base URL(s) and API version
- Authentication model in one line (link to guides/authentication.md)
- Links to the endpoint reference and setup

End with the `docgen` marker:

```markdown
<!-- docgen:auto -->
*Auto-generated. Last updated: <YYYY-MM-DD> from commit `<short-sha>`.*
```

### `docs/architecture.md`

- One-paragraph request lifecycle (client -> router -> service -> data store).
- A Mermaid diagram of the request flow:

   ````markdown
   ```mermaid
   graph LR
       Client -->|HTTP| Router
       Router --> Middleware[Auth / Validation]
       Middleware --> Service
       Service --> DB[(Datastore)]
       Service --> External[3rd-party APIs]
   ```
   ````

- Table of top-level modules with one-line purposes.
- Note cross-cutting middleware (auth, rate limiting, logging).

### `docs/setup.md`

- Prerequisites (runtime versions, system packages)
- Install dependencies
- Required env vars (from `.env.example`) - especially secrets/keys
- Run the service locally
- A sample `curl` call that hits a real endpoint
- Run tests

### `docs/api/*.md` (PRIMARY FOCUS)

Produce a thorough endpoint reference. `api/index.md` lists every endpoint
grouped by resource, with method + path + one-line summary and the auth
requirement.

For each resource group, one page. For EACH endpoint document:
- **Method + path** (e.g. `POST /api/v1/users`)
- **Purpose** - one line
- **Auth** - required scope/role, or public
- **Path / query / body parameters** - name, type, required?, description
- **Request example** - a realistic JSON (or form) body in a fenced block
- **Response example** - the success JSON shape + HTTP status
- **Error responses** - the notable non-2xx codes and when they fire

Use `pymdownx.tabbed` to show request/response side by side where helpful.
Pull the real field names and status codes from the route/handler code -
**don't invent fields**.

### `docs/guides/*.md`

- `authentication.md` - how to obtain and use credentials, token lifetime,
  scopes/roles, refresh flow if any.
- `errors.md` - the canonical error envelope, status-code conventions,
  rate-limit behavior.
- Add others only if clearly warranted (webhooks, pagination, versioning).
extra_instructions: Prioritize the endpoint reference under docs/api/. If you run low on budget, cover fewer endpoints thoroughly rather than many superficially. Always show a concrete request and response example per endpoint.
